titleStmt.title: SII 31.6: original edition by C. A. Padmanabha Sastri and T. S. Ravishankar
No. 6. (A.R. No. B 283 of 1938.) TIPPALŪRU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.

summary.p: On a Slab lying under a tree to the south of the ruined temple of Vīrabhadra.
This record can be assigned to the 7th century A.D. on the basis of palaeography as well as internal evidences.

Registers the grant of fifty marturu of land as pannasa situated in the village Tippalūru to Kiḻvūru Kattiśarmma, a (pāradāya) brāhmin of Bhāradvāja gōtra belonging to Tarkkapuḻōla probably by the king Eṟikallu Muturāju Puṇyakumāruṉṟu at the time of Bṛihaspatihōra, on the second tithi of the dark fortnight (Chīkubidiya) of the month of Kārtīka, Monday, when the nakshatra of the day was Punarupūshaṁbu (punarvasu). The donor king is endowed with titles such as Gaṇyamānuṉṟu, Maṟuntra-piḍugu, Madamuditunṟru, Uttamōttāmunṟu and is said to have been ruling the Rēnaḍu country from the capital Cherpali. The record ends with a statement of Chāmana kālu dharmmam, which indicates the protector of this dharma is Chāmanakālu.
(Pub. Epigraphia Indica. Vol. XXVII, pp 231 ff. Ins. No. F.)

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . Erikala muttu
rāju puṁṇyakumārunṟu gaṇya
mānunṟu maṟantrapiḍugu madamu
ditunṟuttayōttamunṟayina vāṁ
nṟu cepaliya paṭukānu rēnā ṇdē
ḷucu ta¨rkkapuḷōla pāradāya
ki ṟevuru kattiśa¨rmmaku ti[¨r]pavā
rapanāra koṇḍakā¨rttiya cīku
ra biḍiya sōmavāraṁbuṁ puṇyaru
pupyaṁbu bṛhaspati hōrakā
nu Embhadiye cāmaṇakāla [dha¨rmaṁ]
Digital edition of SII 31.6 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
